Pass it as the `world_model` argument of the `python` tool call, alongside `code`. That argument is the one that carries forward, so send it on every call, even when you reply with a tool call and no assistant text.

Record every unproductive probe in failed_probes so it is never repeated, and tag claims VERIFIED(step N) or HYPOTHESIS.

- Object taxonomy: {'purple_ring': 'hollow diamond ring at ~21,39', 'white_diamond': 'white diamond that follows clicks, moves to clicked position', 'gray_path': 'reconfigurable diagonal path', 'purple_square': 'purple square with magenta center at [56,27]', 'dark_gray_diamond': 'dark gray diamond at [60,27]'}
- Win pattern: HYPOTHESIS: Continue oscillating clicks until level completes

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Looking at the image and the output:
- Dark gray (G) is at (21, 39) - the ring position ✓
- Purple square (p) is at (30, 36) - moved up from (40, 33)!
- White diamond (W) is at (40, 33) - where purple was
So clicking purple moved it UP along the path, but not all the way to the ring. It moved from (40, 33) to (30, 36). The dark gray is already in the ring.
I need to click purple again to move it further up to the ring.
